signals.idea: Polish and integrate video editor into Reigh: full-screen editor page (hide GlobalHeader, use all vertical space), suppress EditorPane on editor route, drag from GenerationsPane into timeline/assets (variant-level), assets panel collapsed by default, generation variant click-through, aesthetic polish

unresolved_flags.concern: The plan violates the required flat step structure. It uses phase headers with nested step headings instead of flat `## Step N:` sections.

Pathname-only full-screen gating would also hide chrome for the timeline list view on `/tools/video-editor`, which conflicts with the plan's own assumption that only the active editor shell should get the full-screen treatment.

The full-screen layout steps miss repo-level wrappers that still constrain the editor: the route outlet sits inside a centered `container mx-auto` shell, and the global social footer still renders below the page. Hiding the header and pane padding alone will not produce a true full-screen editor.

The drag plan targets the wrong source component and introduces a parallel drag protocol. Gallery drag starts in `MediaGalleryItem`, not `DraggableImage`, and existing gallery-to-shot drops already use the shared generation drag helpers.

The plan assumes GenerationsPane items already operate at generation-variant level, but the current pane is backed by project generations, not `generation_variants`. Variant-level drag needs a data-model/query change first, not just extra `dataTransfer` fields.

The proposed 'register asset' drop flow is not compatible with the current persistence model. `patchRegistry` only mutates local editor state; it does not persist `asset_registry`, and `saveTimeline` writes only timeline config.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 10.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 6.

items: Video editor page uses full viewport height — no GlobalHeader visible, no EditorPane dots, no pane-margin padding
Navigating away from /tools/video-editor restores normal layout (GlobalHeader, panes, margins)
A minimal back bar (~32-40px) shows at top of editor with navigation link and timeline name
Generation variants can be dragged from GenerationsPane and dropped onto the timeline to create clips
Generation variants can be dragged onto the assets panel to register without creating a clip
Dropped variant assets include generationId and variantId in their AssetRegistryEntry
Existing drag flows (asset-key from panel, file upload) continue working unchanged
Assets panel in PropertiesPanel is collapsed by default
Assets with generationId/variantId show a click-through action that opens MediaLightbox to the source generation+variant
No hardcoded color classes (zinc-*, gray-*, slate-*) remain in video editor components — all use theme tokens
Editor visually matches Reigh's dark theme and feels like a native tool
Compact mode in EditorPane still works on non-editor routes
npm run build passes with no type errors

unresolved_flags.concern: The plan violates the required flat step structure. It uses phase headers with nested step headings instead of flat `## Step N:` sections.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The user explicitly required flat h2 headings (`## Step N:`) with no phase headers pushing steps to h3. The submitted plan uses `## Phase 1/2/3` and `### Step 1/2/...`.

unresolved_flags.concern: Pathname-only full-screen gating would also hide chrome for the timeline list view on `/tools/video-editor`, which conflicts with the plan's own assumption that only the active editor shell should get the full-screen treatment.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/pages/VideoEditorPage.tsx:206-223` serves both states from the same pathname: without `?timeline=...` it renders `TimelineList`, and with `?timeline=...` it renders `VideoEditorShell`. A `pathname === '/tools/video-editor'` check in layout code cannot distinguish those two states.

unresolved_flags.concern: The full-screen layout steps miss repo-level wrappers that still constrain the editor: the route outlet sits inside a centered `container mx-auto` shell, and the global social footer still renders below the page. Hiding the header and pane padding alone will not produce a true full-screen editor.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/app/components/LayoutMainContent.tsx:64-67` wraps all routed pages in `<main className="container mx-auto ...">`. `src/app/Layout.tsx:128-134` always renders `SocialIcons`, and `src/app/components/SocialIcons.tsx:9-31` adds vertical footer space. The plan does not account for either wrapper.

unresolved_flags.concern: The drag plan targets the wrong source component and introduces a parallel drag protocol. Gallery drag starts in `MediaGalleryItem`, not `DraggableImage`, and existing gallery-to-shot drops already use the shared generation drag helpers.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:324-326` attaches `draggable` and `onDragStart`, and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem.tsx:267-273` calls `setGenerationDragData(...)`. `src/shared/components/DraggableImage.tsx:10-15` is only a non-draggable wrapper around children. The shared drag contract lives in `src/shared/lib/dnd/dragDrop.ts:123-158`, and shot drop targets consume it in `src/shared/components/ShotImageManager/components/BatchDropZone.tsx:315-324`.

unresolved_flags.concern: The plan assumes GenerationsPane items already operate at generation-variant level, but the current pane is backed by project generations, not `generation_variants`. Variant-level drag needs a data-model/query change first, not just extra `dataTransfer` fields.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/features/gallery/hooks/useGalleryPageState.ts:67-109` calls `useProjectGenerations(...)` with the normal generations path. In `src/shared/hooks/projects/useProjectGenerations.ts:249-257`, the `generation_variants` query is only used when `filters.variantsOnly` is set. The default generation transform is `transformGeneration` in `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:263-375`; variant rows are only produced by `transformVariant` in `src/shared/lib/generationTransformers.ts:438-475`.

unresolved_flags.concern: The proposed 'register asset' drop flow is not compatible with the current persistence model. `patchRegistry` only mutates local editor state; it does not persist `asset_registry`, and `saveTimeline` writes only timeline config.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineData.ts:339-390` shows `patchRegistry(...)` updating local state and calling `commitData(..., { save: false })`. `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:36-52` saves only the `config` column, while `src/tools/video-editor/data/SupabaseDataProvider.ts:87-131` is the existing code path that persists `asset_registry` to Supabase.
